title: Rich Irish Chicken Liver Grill
description: A quick, hearty Irish-style dish featuring tender chicken livers marinated in Guinness, smoked paprika and herbs, then grilled to perfection.
instructions: Preheat a grill pan or griddle over medium-high heat.
Clean the livers: trim any fat or connective tissue and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, Guinness, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, smoked paprika, garlic powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Coat the livers in the mixture, cover, and let marinate for 15–20 minutes at room temperature.
Place the marinated livers onto the hot grill pan; cook for 2–3 minutes per side until a slight crust forms and the interior is just pink (internal temperature 74°C/165°F).
Transfer the cooked livers to a plate, add a pat of butter on top, and let them rest for a minute while the butter melts.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve immediately with a side of mashed potatoes or soda bread if desired.
